name: WorkMesh
tagline: Docs-first project and task orchestration for developers and coding agents.

use_for: shared CLI and MCP workflow surface

steps: say: Bootstrap WorkMesh in this repo. Use MCP if available, otherwise CLI.

say: Use WorkMesh for this feature end to end. Create/update PRD, create and maintain tasks with acceptance criteria and definition of done, keep context current, and track stable decisions in Truth Ledger.

bootstrap_behavior.modern_workmesh_layout: validate health, show context, and suggest next work
bootstrap_behavior.legacy_layout: migrate to modern layout and continue
bootstrap_behavior.clone_based_workflow: keep work moving and recommend worktree consolidation

cli_usage.tool_info_note: Shared metadata, examples, and response-contract guidance come from workmesh-tools. The MCP registry is still the canonical source for full MCP input schemas.

tasks.definition_of_done_rule: Definition of Done must be outcome-based, not just hygiene items.

truth_ledger.purpose: durable decisions for humans and agents

workstreams.summary: parallel work model, usually one active workstream per git worktree

architecture.workmesh-core: domain logic, storage safety, repo/global state
architecture.workmesh-tools: shared tool metadata, response-policy helpers, and adapter-neutral tooling helpers
architecture.workmesh-mcp: stdio wrapper around the MCP adapter

contributor_rules: start shared tool metadata, response-policy, and adapter-neutral tooling changes in workmesh-tools
put CLI-only parsing or presentation changes in workmesh
put MCP transport-only changes in workmesh-mcp-server
do not make the CLI depend on workmesh-mcp-server

mutation_response_contract.goal: save tokens by keeping write responses compact
mutation_response_contract.verbose_opt_in: pass verbose=true for richer post-write state

guarantees: lock-safe and atomic writes for critical tracking files

changelog_discipline: move relevant Unreleased items into a new versioned CHANGELOG section
update compare links at the bottom of CHANGELOG.md
docs_sync_rule: keep both changes in the same commit when install, setup, commands, layout, or roadmap changes
